originalText: כָּעֵת בַּמָּרוֹם תַּמְרִיא תִּשְׂחַק לַסּוּס וּלְרֹכְבוֹ׃
englishText: When the time cometh, she raiseth her wings on high, And scorneth the horse and his rider.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הֲתִתֵּן לַסּוּס גְּבוּרָה הֲתַלְבִּישׁ צַוָּארוֹ רַעְמָה׃
englishText: Hast thou given the horse his strength? Hast thou clothed his neck with fierceness?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הְתַרְעִישֶׁנּוּ כָּאַרְבֶּה הוֹד נַחְרוֹ אֵימָה׃
englishText: Hast thou made him to leap as a locust? The glory of his snorting is terrible.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יַחְפְּרוּ בָעֵמֶק וְיָשִׂישׂ בְּכֹחַ יֵצֵא לִקְרַאת־נָשֶׁק׃
englishText: He paweth in the valley, and rejoiceth in his strength; He goeth out to meet the clash of arms.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יִשְׂחַק לְפַחַד וְלֹא יֵחָת וְלֹא־יָשׁוּב מִפְּנֵי־חָרֶב׃
englishText: He mocketh at fear, and is not affrighted; Neither turneth he back from the sword.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עָלָיו תִּרְנֶה אַשְׁפָּה לַהַב חֲנִית וְכִידוֹן׃
englishText: The quiver rattleth upon him, The glittering spear and the javelin.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: בְּרַעַשׁ וְרֹגֶז יְגַמֶּא־אָרֶץ וְלֹא־יַאֲמִין כִּי־קוֹל שׁוֹפָר׃
englishText: He swalloweth the ground with storm and rage; Neither believeth he that it is the voice of the horn.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: בְּדֵי שֹׁפָר יֹאמַר הֶאָח וּמֵרָחוֹק יָרִיחַ מִלְחָמָה רַעַם שָׂרִים וּתְרוּעָה׃
englishText: As oft as he heareth the horn he saith: ‘Ha, ha! ’ And he smelleth the battle afar off, The thunder of the captains, and the shouting.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הֲ‍מִבִּינָתְךָ יַאֲבֶר־נֵץ יִפְרֹשׂ כנפו [כְּנָפָיו] לְתֵימָן׃
englishText: Doth the hawk soar by thy wisdom, And stretch her wings toward the south?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אִם־עַל־פִּיךָ יַגְבִּיהַּ נָשֶׁר וְכִי יָרִים קִנּוֹ׃
englishText: Doth the vulture mount up at thy command, And make her nest on high?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: סֶלַע יִשְׁכֹּן וְיִתְלֹנָן עַל־שֶׁן־סֶלַע וּמְצוּדָה׃
englishText: She dwelleth and abideth on the rock, Upon the crag of the rock, and the stronghold.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִשָּׁם חָפַר־אֹכֶל לְמֵרָחוֹק עֵינָיו יַבִּיטוּ׃
englishText: From thence she spieth out the prey; Her eyes behold it afar off.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: ואפרחו [וְאֶפְרֹחָיו] יְעַלְעוּ־דָם וּבַאֲשֶׁר חֲלָלִים שָׁם הוּא׃
englishText: Her young ones also suck up blood; And where the slain are, there is she.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיַּעַן יְהוָה אֶת־אִיּוֹב וַיֹּאמַר׃
englishText: Moreover the LORD answered Job, and said:
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הֲרֹב עִם־שַׁדַּי יִסּוֹר מוֹכִיחַ אֱלוֹהַּ יַעֲנֶנָּה׃
englishText: Shall he that reproveth contend with the Almighty? He that argueth with God, let him answer it.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיַּעַן אִיּוֹב אֶת־יְהוָה וַיֹּאמַר׃
englishText: Then Job answered the LORD, and said:
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הֵן קַלֹּתִי מָה אֲשִׁיבֶךָּ יָדִי שַׂמְתִּי לְמוֹ־פִי׃
englishText: Behold, I am of small account; what shall I answer Thee? I lay my hand upon my mouth.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אַחַת דִּבַּרְתִּי וְלֹא אֶעֱנֶה וּשְׁתַּיִם וְלֹא אוֹסִיף׃
englishText: Once have I spoken, but I will not answer again; Yea, twice, but I will proceed no further.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיַּעַן־יְהוָה אֶת־אִיּוֹב מנ סערה [מִן ] [סְעָרָה] וַיֹּאמַר׃
englishText: Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind, and said:
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֱזָר־נָא כְגֶבֶר חֲלָצֶיךָ אֶשְׁאָלְךָ וְהוֹדִיעֵנִי׃
englishText: Gird up thy loins now like a man; I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto Me. .
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הַאַף תָּפֵר מִשְׁפָּטִי תַּרְשִׁיעֵנִי לְמַעַן תִּצְדָּק׃
englishText: Wilt thou even make void My judgment? Wilt thou condemn Me, that thou mayest be justified?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וְאִם־זְרוֹעַ כָּאֵל לָךְ וּבְקוֹל כָּמֹהוּ תַרְעֵם׃
englishText: Or hast thou an arm like God? And canst thou thunder with a voice like Him?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עֲדֵה נָא גָאוֹן וָגֹבַהּ וְהוֹד וְהָדָר תִּלְבָּשׁ׃
englishText: Deck thyself now with majesty and excellency, And array thyself with glory and beauty.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הָפֵץ עֶבְרוֹת אַפֶּךָ וּרְאֵה כָל־גֵּאֶה וְהַשְׁפִּילֵהוּ׃
englishText: Cast abroad the rage of thy wrath; And look upon every one that is proud, and abase him.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: רְאֵה כָל־גֵּאֶה הַכְנִיעֵהוּ וַהֲדֹךְ רְשָׁעִים תַּחְתָּם׃
englishText: Look on every one that is proud, and bring him low; And tread down the wicked in their place.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: טָמְנֵם בֶּעָפָר יָחַד פְּנֵיהֶם חֲבֹשׁ בַּטָּמוּן׃
englishText: Hide them in the dust together; Bind their faces in the hidden place.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וְגַם־אֲנִי אוֹדֶךָּ כִּי־תוֹשִׁעַ לְךָ יְמִינֶךָ׃
englishText: Then will I also confess unto thee That thine own right hand can save thee.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הִנֵּה־נָא בְהֵמוֹת אֲשֶׁר־עָשִׂיתִי עִמָּךְ חָצִיר כַּבָּקָר יֹאכֵל׃
englishText: Behold now behemoth, which I made with thee; He eateth grass as an ox.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הִנֵּה־נָא כֹחוֹ בְמָתְנָיו וְאֹנוֹ בִּשְׁרִירֵי בִטְנוֹ׃
englishText: Lo now, his strength is in his loins, And his force is in the stays of his body.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יַחְפֹּץ זְנָבוֹ כְמוֹ־אָרֶז גִּידֵי פחדו [פַחֲדָיו] יְשֹׂרָגוּ׃
englishText: He straineth his tail like a cedar; The sinews of his thighs are knit together.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עֲצָמָיו אֲפִיקֵי נְחוּשָׁה גְּרָמָיו כִּמְטִיל בַּרְזֶל׃
englishText: His bones are as pipes of brass; His gristles are like bars of iron.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הוּא רֵאשִׁית דַּרְכֵי־אֵל הָעֹשׂוֹ יַגֵּשׁ חַרְבּוֹ׃
englishText: He is the beginning of the ways of God; He only that made him can make His sword to approach unto him.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כִּי־בוּל הָרִים יִשְׂאוּ־לוֹ וְכָל־חַיַּת הַשָּׂדֶה יְשַׂחֲקוּ־שָׁם׃
englishText: Surely the mountains bring him forth food, And all the beasts of the field play there.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: תַּחַת־צֶאֱלִים יִשְׁכָּב בְּסֵתֶר קָנֶה וּבִצָּה׃
englishText: He lieth under the lotus-trees, In the covert of the reed, and fens.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יְסֻכֻּהוּ צֶאֱלִים צִלֲלוֹ יְסֻבּוּהוּ עַרְבֵי־נָחַל׃
englishText: The lotus-trees cover him with their shadow; The willows of the brook compass him about.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הֵן יַעֲשֹׁק נָהָר לֹא יַחְפּוֹז יִבְטַח כִּי־יָגִיחַ יַרְדֵּן אֶל־פִּיהוּ׃
englishText: Behold, if a river overflow, he trembleth not; He is confident, though the Jordan rush forth to his mouth.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: בְּעֵינָיו יִקָּחֶנּוּ בְּמוֹקְשִׁים יִנְקָב־אָף׃
englishText: Shall any take him by his eyes, Or pierce through his nose with a snare?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: תִּמְשֹׁךְ לִוְיָתָן בְּחַכָּה וּבְחֶבֶל תַּשְׁקִיעַ לְשֹׁנוֹ׃
englishText: Canst thou draw out leviathan with a fish-hook? Or press down his tongue with a cord?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הֲתָשִׂים אַגְמוֹן בְּאַפּוֹ וּבְחוֹחַ תִּקּוֹב לֶחֱיוֹ׃
englishText: Canst thou put a ring into his nose? Or bore his jaw through with a hook?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הֲיַרְבֶּה אֵלֶיךָ תַּחֲנוּנִים אִם־יְדַבֵּר אֵלֶיךָ רַכּוֹת׃
englishText: Will he make many supplications unto thee? Or will he speak soft words unto thee?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הֲיִכְרֹת בְּרִית עִמָּךְ תִּקָּחֶנּוּ לְעֶבֶד עוֹלָם׃
englishText: Will he make a covenant with thee, That thou shouldest take him for a servant for ever?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הַתְשַׂחֶק־בּוֹ כַּצִּפּוֹר וְתִקְשְׁרֶנּוּ לְנַעֲרוֹתֶיךָ׃
englishText: Wilt thou play with him as with a bird? Or wilt thou bind him for thy maidens? .
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יִכְרוּ עָלָיו חַבָּרִים יֶחֱצוּהוּ בֵּין כְּנַעֲנִים׃
englishText: Will the bands of fishermen make a banquet of him? Will they part him among the merchants?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הַתְמַלֵּא בְשֻׂכּוֹת עוֹרוֹ וּבְצִלְצַל דָּגִים רֹאשׁוֹ׃
englishText: Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons? Or his head with fish-spears?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שִׂים־עָלָיו כַּפֶּךָ זְכֹר מִלְחָמָה אַל־תּוֹסַף׃
englishText: Lay thy hand upon him; Think upon the battle, thou wilt do so no more.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הֵן־תֹּחַלְתּוֹ נִכְזָבָה הֲגַם אֶל־מַרְאָיו יֻטָל׃
englishText: Behold, the hope of him is in vain; Shall not one be cast down even at the sight of him?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לֹא־אַכְזָר כִּי יְעוּרֶנּוּ וּמִי הוּא לְפָנַי יִתְיַצָּב׃
englishText: None is so fierce that dare stir him up; Who then is able to stand before Me?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִי הִקְדִּימַנִי וַאֲשַׁלֵּם תַּחַת כָּל־הַשָּׁמַיִם לִי־הוּא׃
englishText: Who hath given Me anything beforehand, that I should repay him? Whatsoever is under the whole heaven is Mine.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לא־[לוֹ־] אַחֲרִישׁ בַּדָּיו וּדְבַר־גְּבוּרוֹת וְחִין עֶרְכּוֹ׃
englishText: Would I keep silence concerning his boastings, or his proud talk, or his fair array of words?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִי־גִלָּה פְּנֵי לְבוּשׁוֹ בְּכֶפֶל רִסְנוֹ מִי יָבוֹא׃
englishText: Who can uncover the face of his garment? Who shall come within his double bridle?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: דַּלְתֵי פָנָיו מִי פִתֵּחַ סְבִיבוֹת שִׁנָּיו אֵימָה׃
englishText: Who can open the doors of his face? Round about his teeth is terror.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: גַּאֲוָה אֲפִיקֵי מָגִנִּים סָגוּר חוֹתָם צָר׃
englishText: His scales are his pride, shut up together as with a close seal.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֶחָד בְּאֶחָד יִגַּשׁוּ וְרוּחַ לֹא־יָבוֹא בֵינֵיהֶם׃
englishText: One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אִישׁ־בְּאָחִיהוּ יְדֻבָּקוּ יִתְלַכְּדוּ וְלֹא יִתְפָּרָדוּ׃
englishText: They are joined one to another; They stick together, that they cannot be sundered.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עֲ‍טִישֹׁתָיו תָּהֶל אוֹר וְעֵינָיו כְּעַפְעַפֵּי־שָׁחַר׃
englishText: His sneezings flash forth light, and his eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִפִּיו לַפִּידִים יַהֲלֹכוּ כִּידוֹדֵי אֵשׁ יִתְמַלָּטוּ׃
englishText: Out of his mouth go burning torches, and sparks of fire leap forth.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִנְּחִירָיו יֵצֵא עָשָׁן כְּדוּד נָפוּחַ וְאַגְמֹן׃
englishText: Out of his nostrils goeth smoke, as out of a seething pot and burning rushes.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: נַפְשׁוֹ גֶּחָלִים תְּלַהֵט וְלַהַב מִפִּיו יֵצֵא׃
englishText: His breath kindleth coals, and a flame goeth out of his mouth.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: בְּצַוָּארוֹ יָלִין עֹז וּלְפָנָיו תָּדוּץ דְּאָבָה׃
englishText: In his neck abideth strength, and dismay danceth before him.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מַפְּלֵי בְשָׂרוֹ דָבֵקוּ יָצוּק עָלָיו בַּל־יִמּוֹט׃
englishText: The flakes of his flesh are joined together; they are firm upon him; they cannot be moved.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לִבּוֹ יָצוּק כְּמוֹ־אָבֶן וְיָצוּק כְּפֶלַח תַּחְתִּית׃
englishText: His heart is as firm as a stone; yea, firm as the nether millstone.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִשֵּׂתוֹ יָגוּרוּ אֵלִים מִשְּׁבָרִים יִתְחַטָּאוּ׃
englishText: When he raiseth himself up, the mighty are afraid; by reason of despair they are beside themselves.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מַשִּׂיגֵהוּ חֶרֶב בְּלִי תָקוּם חֲנִית מַסָּע וְשִׁרְיָה׃
englishText: If one lay at him with the sword, it will not hold; nor the spear, the dart, nor the pointed shaft.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יַחְשֹׁב לְתֶבֶן בַּרְזֶל לְעֵץ רִקָּבוֹן נְחוּשָׁה׃
englishText: He esteemeth iron as straw, and brass as rotten wood.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לֹא־יַבְרִיחֶנּוּ בֶן־קָשֶׁת לְקַשׁ נֶהְפְּכוּ־לוֹ אַבְנֵי־קָלַע׃
englishText: The arrow cannot make him flee; slingstones are turned with him into stubble.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כְּקַשׁ נֶחְשְׁבוּ תוֹתָח וְיִשְׂחַק לְרַעַשׁ כִּידוֹן׃
englishText: Clubs are accounted as stubble; He laugheth at the rattling of the javelin.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: תַּחְתָּיו חַדּוּדֵי חָרֶשׂ יִרְפַּד חָרוּץ עֲלֵי־טִיט׃
englishText: Sharpest potsherds are under him; He spreadeth a threshing-sledge upon the mire.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יַרְתִּיחַ כַּסִּיר מְצוּלָה יָם יָשִׂים כַּמֶּרְקָחָה׃
englishText: He maketh the deep to boil like a pot; He maketh the sea like a seething mixture.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אַחֲרָיו יָאִיר נָתִיב יַחְשֹׁב תְּהוֹם לְשֵׂיבָה׃
englishText: He maketh a path to shine after him; One would think the deep to be hoary.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֵין־עַל־עָפָר מָשְׁלוֹ הֶעָשׂוּ לִבְלִי־חָת׃
englishText: Upon earth there is not his like who is made to be fearless.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֵת־כָּל־גָּבֹהַּ יִרְאֶה הוּא מֶלֶךְ עַל־כָּל־בְּנֵי־שָׁחַץ׃
englishText: He looketh at all high things; He is king over all the proud beasts.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיַּעַן אִיּוֹב אֶת־יְהוָה וַיֹּאמַר׃
englishText: Then Job answered the LORD, and said:
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: ידעת [יָדַעְתִּי] כִּי־כֹל תּוּכָל וְלֹא־יִבָּצֵר מִמְּךָ מְזִמָּה׃
englishText: I know that Thou canst do every thing, And that no purpose can be withholden from Thee.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִי זֶה מַעְלִים עֵצָה בְּלִי דָעַת לָכֵן הִגַּדְתִּי וְלֹא אָבִין נִפְלָאוֹת מִמֶּנִּי וְלֹא אֵדָע׃
englishText: Who is this that hideth counsel without knowledge? Therefore have I uttered that which I understood not, Things too wonderful for me, which I knew not.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שְׁמַע־נָא וְאָנֹכִי אֲדַבֵּר אֶשְׁאָלְךָ וְהוֹדִיעֵנִי׃
englishText: Hear, I beseech Thee, and I will speak; I will demand of Thee, and declare Thou unto me.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לְשֵׁמַע־אֹזֶן שְׁמַעְתִּיךָ וְעַתָּה עֵינִי רָאָתְךָ׃
englishText: I had heard of Thee by the hearing of the ear; But now mine eye seeth Thee;
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עַל־כֵּן אֶמְאַס וְנִחַמְתִּי עַל־עָפָר וָאֵפֶר׃
englishText: Wherefore I abhor my words, and repent, Seeing I am dust and ashes.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיְהִי אַחַר דִּבֶּר יְהוָה אֶת־הַדְּבָרִים הָאֵלֶּה אֶל־אִיּוֹב וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־אֱלִיפַז הַתֵּימָנִי חָרָה אַפִּי בְךָ וּבִשְׁנֵי רֵעֶיךָ כִּי לֹא דִבַּרְתֶּם אֵלַי נְכוֹנָה כְּעַבְדִּי אִיּוֹב׃
englishText: And it was so, that after the LORD had spoken these words unto Job, the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite: ‘My wrath is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends; for ye have not spoken of Me the thing that is right, as My servant Job hath.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וְעַתָּה קְחוּ־לָכֶם שִׁבְעָה־פָרִים וְשִׁבְעָה אֵילִים וּלְכוּ אֶל־עַבְדִּי אִיּוֹב וְהַעֲלִיתֶם עוֹלָה בַּעַדְכֶם וְאִיּוֹב עַבְדִּי יִתְפַּלֵּל עֲלֵיכֶם כִּי אִם־פָּנָיו אֶשָּׂא לְבִלְתִּי עֲשׂוֹת עִמָּכֶם נְבָלָה כִּי לֹא דִבַּרְתֶּם אֵלַי נְכוֹנָה כְּעַבְדִּי אִיּוֹב׃
englishText: Now therefore, take unto you seven bullocks and seven rams, and go to My servant Job, and offer up for yourselves a burnt-offering; and My servant Job shall pray for you; for him will I accept, that I do not unto you aught unseemly; for ye have not spoken of Me the thing that is right, as my servant Job hath.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיֵּלְכוּ אֱלִיפַז הַתֵּימָנִי וּבִלְדַּד הַשּׁוּחִי צֹפַר הַנַּעֲמָתִי וַיַּעֲשׂוּ כַּאֲשֶׁר דִּבֶּר אֲלֵיהֶם יְהוָה וַיִּשָּׂא יְהוָה אֶת־פְּנֵי אִיּוֹב׃
englishText: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went, and did according as the LORD commanded them; and the LORD accepted Job.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיהוָה שָׁב אֶת־שבית [שְׁבוּת] אִיּוֹב בְּהִתְפַּלְלוֹ בְּעַד רֵעֵהוּ וַיֹּסֶף יְהוָה אֶת־כָּל־אֲשֶׁר לְאִיּוֹב לְמִשְׁנֶה׃
englishText: And the LORD changed the fortune of Job, when he prayed for his friends; and the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיָּבֹאוּ אֵלָיו כָּל־אֶחָיו וְכָל־אחיתיו [אַחְיוֹתָיו] וְכָל־יֹדְעָיו לְפָנִים וַיֹּאכְלוּ עִמּוֹ לֶחֶם בְּבֵיתוֹ וַיָּנֻדוּ לוֹ וַיְנַחֲמוּ אֹתוֹ עַל כָּל־הָרָעָה אֲשֶׁר־הֵבִיא יְהוָה עָלָיו וַיִּתְּנוּ־לוֹ אִישׁ קְשִׂיטָה אֶחָת וְאִישׁ נֶזֶם זָהָב אֶחָד׃
englishText: Then came there unto him all his brethren, and all his sisters, and all they that had been of his acquaintance before, and did eat bread with him in his house; and they bemoaned him, and comforted him concerning all the evil that the LORD had brought upon him; every man also gave him a piece of money, and every one a ring of gold.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיהוָה בֵּרַךְ אֶת־אַחֲרִית אִיּוֹב מֵרֵאשִׁתוֹ וַיְהִי־לוֹ אַרְבָּעָה עָשָׂר אֶלֶף צֹאן וְשֵׁשֶׁת אֲלָפִים גְּמַלִּים וְאֶלֶף־צֶמֶד בָּקָר וְאֶלֶף אֲתוֹנוֹת׃
englishText: So the LORD blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning; and he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she-asses. .
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיְהִי־לוֹ שִׁבְעָנָה בָנִים וְשָׁלוֹשׁ בָּנוֹת׃
englishText: He had also seven sons and three daughters.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיִּקְרָא שֵׁם־הָאַחַת יְמִימָה וְשֵׁם הַשֵּׁנִית קְצִיעָה וְשֵׁם הַשְּׁלִישִׁית קֶרֶן הַפּוּךְ׃
englishText: And he called the name of the first, Jemimah; and the name of the second, Keziah; and the name of the third, Keren-happuch.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וְלֹא נִמְצָא נָשִׁים יָפוֹת כִּבְנוֹת אִיּוֹב בְּכָל־הָאָרֶץ וַיִּתֵּן לָהֶם אֲבִיהֶם נַחֲלָה בְּתוֹךְ אֲחֵיהֶם׃
englishText: And in all the land were no women found so fair as the daughters of Job; and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיְחִי אִיּוֹב אַחֲרֵי־זֹאת מֵאָה וְאַרְבָּעִים שָׁנָה וירא [וַיִּרְאֶה] אֶת־בָּנָיו וְאֶת־בְּנֵי בָנָיו אַרְבָּעָה דֹּרוֹת׃
englishText: And after this Job lived a hundred and forty years, and saw his sons, and his sons’sons, even four generations.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וַיָּמָת אִיּוֹב זָקֵן וּשְׂבַע יָמִים׃
englishText: So Job died, being old and full of days.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)
